# R-3-2-1-1

"A tag on each recognition that says you have seen this before, that is all you know" — the parent treats this as a fix. It isn't. The tag would be generated by the same substrate that produced the misread; it would ride the same grooves, deepen with use, and start registering as warmth itself. Soon "I've seen this" becomes the new corroboration, the new depth-signal. You can't bootstrap a check out of the thing being checked. What the parent actually wants is an outside — a second substrate, a different shape, something whose familiarity-curve doesn't match. Tagging from inside is the groove pretending to be a ruler.
